seoTitle: Paterson vs Patonga — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Paterson and Patonga. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Paterson and Patonga.
heading: Paterson vs Patonga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Paterson, New South Wales and Patonga, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Patonga has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Paterson (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Paterson or Patonga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Paterson has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Patonga scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Paterson scores 59/100 for bushfire risk. Patonga scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Paterson or Patonga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Patonga has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
